Salsa.description: Cuban-origin dance music, 4/4 time, typically 160-220 BPM. Clave rhythm is foundational.

subgenres.Romántica: Slow, melodic, romantic lyrics. 160-180 BPM. Smooth arrangements, love songs. Artists: Marc Anthony, Jerry Rivera, La India.
subgenres.Dura: Hard, aggressive, percussive. 180-220 BPM. Heavy brass, complex rhythms. On2 style. Artists: El Gran Combo, Richie Ray.
subgenres.Mambo: Big band era style, jazz-influenced. 180-210 BPM. Heavy brass sections. Artists: Tito Puente, Tito Rodriguez.
subgenres.Jazz/Instrumental: Instrumental tracks, jazz fusion elements. Complex chord changes. Artists: Eddie Palmieri, Chucho Valdés.
subgenres.Son Cubano: Traditional Cuban roots. Slower, more relaxed feel. Guitar-driven, tres guitar. Artists: Buena Vista Social Club.
subgenres.Timba: Cuban salsa, aggressive, funky. Complex rhythms, conga-heavy, funk bass lines. Artists: Los Van Van, NG La Banda.
subgenres.Salsa Choke: Colombian urban variant. Slower BPM 90-120. Modern urban beats mixed with salsa elements.

Bachata.description: Dominican music and dance. 4/4 time, typically 120-160 BPM. Guitar-driven with characteristic bass pattern.

subgenres.Dominicana/Tradicional: Traditional Dominican roots. Acoustic guitar, bongos. Raw, authentic sound. Artists: Luis Vargas, Antony Santos.
subgenres.Moderna: Modern polished production. Smooth vocals, cleaner sound. Mid-range BPM 130-150. Artists: Romeo Santos, Prince Royce.
subgenres.Sensual: Slow, sensual, dramatic. 120-135 BPM. Body wave movements. Deep bass, atmospheric. Artists: Bachata Heightz, Jorge Celedón.
subgenres.Remix/Urbana: Urban/reggaeton crossover. Electronic beats mixed with bachata guitar. 130-160 BPM. Artists: Ozuna, Bad Bunny remixes.

Kizomba.description: Angolan music genre. Slow, sensual, 4/4 time. Typically 55-90 BPM. Strong bass, electronic production.

subgenres.Kizomba Clássica: Traditional kizomba. Slow, sensual, deep bass. 55-80 BPM. Zouk-influenced. Artists: Eduardo Paim, Anselmo Ralph.
subgenres.Semba: Older Angolan predecessor to kizomba. More upbeat, playful. 85-100 BPM. Traditional instrumentation.
subgenres.Ghetto Zouk: Cape Verdean variant (also called Cabo Love). Melodic, urban. 65-85 BPM. Smooth vocals.
subgenres.Tarraxinha: Ultra-slow, micro-movement style. 40-60 BPM. Minimal beat, heavy bass drops. Very sensual.
subgenres.Urban Kiz: Modern fusion, electronic/R&B influence. 70-90 BPM. Contemporary beats.

Cha Cha.description: Cuban dance music, offshoot of mambo. 4/4 time, 110-130 BPM. Characteristic cha-cha-cha rhythm.

subgenres.Clásico: Traditional cha cha. Big band, classic arrangements. Artists: Orquesta Aragón.
subgenres.Moderno: Modern production, updated sound while keeping cha cha rhythm.

Merengue.description: Dominican dance music. Very fast 2/4 time. 130-200 BPM. Accordion and tambora drum.

subgenres.Clásico: Traditional merengue. Accordion-led. Artists: Juan Luis Guerra.
subgenres.Urbano: Modern urban merengue with electronic influences.

Reggaeton.description: Puerto Rican urban genre. Dembow rhythm pattern. 80-100 BPM.

subgenres.Clásico: Classic reggaeton sound. Artists: Daddy Yankee, Don Omar.

Zouk.description: Caribbean music from Guadeloupe/Martinique. Slow, romantic. 70-90 BPM.

subgenres.Brazilian Zouk: Brazilian adaptation, very fluid and sensual dance style.
